vitals.location: Exeter Racecourse, Kennford, Exeter, Devon, EX6 7XS, England

climate.description: Southwest England summer with Dartmoor microclimate. Mild temps but sudden rain, fog, and headwinds common.

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 79. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 74.

course_description.character: Multiple loops across Dartmoor from a start/finish at Exeter Racecourse, with a principal feed station at Postbridge passed three times over the roughly 200-mile / 323 km route. The course has moved to this Exeter-based configuration from an earlier planned layout; the organizer describes an especially difficult final climb without naming it in current material. Elevation gain is reported inconsistently by the organizer (18,000 ft alongside both 5,500 m and an apparently miscalculated 6,000 m) — 18,000 ft / roughly 5,500 m is the more defensible figure.
course_description.signature_challenge: The 19-hour time limit itself — 05:00 to midnight, averaging roughly 10.5 mph including every stop, with intermediate cutoffs that can retire riders who fall behind.
suffering_zones: Postbridge — passed three times as the principal feed station, a recurring psychological checkpoint
Repeated Dartmoor loops — the moorland terrain compounds across multiple passes rather than a single point-to-point route
The unnamed final climb the organizer describes as especially difficult, arriving deep into a very long day

logistics.lodging_strategy: Camping available at event village. Exeter hotels 5 miles away. Local B&Bs in Kennford.
logistics.transport: Exeter Racecourse HQ just off M5/A38 junction. 5AM mass start, 19-hour cutoff to midnight. Bag drop at Postbridge feedstation. GPS tracking enables dot-watching. Free parking, camping available.

history.origin_story: Part of Moor to Sea Sportive revival. Dartmoor Legend established as UK's toughest one-day sportive, claiming superiority over Yorkshire Beast and Dragon Ride.

biased_opinion.summary: Dartmoor Legend calls itself the UK's toughest sportive — treat that as the organizer's own marketing claim, not an independently verified fact, though BikeRadar has included it among the UK's toughest long-distance events. What's verifiable: roughly 200 miles and 18,000 ft of climbing across repeated Dartmoor loops, a strict 19-hour cutoff, and a field that stays small relative to mainstream sportives — 2025 recorded 28 full-route finishers from 43 entrants. The event's history is rockier than its current branding suggests: a planned 2021 inaugural edition was cancelled, and the first edition that actually ran was in 2022. The organizer's own web presence is untidy — conflicting dates and elevation figures across different pages — which matters more here than on a bigger, better-resourced event.
strengths: A genuinely long, hard single-day ultra format — roughly 200 miles / 18,000 ft across Dartmoor
Full support for the distance: five feed stations, RFID timing, GPS tracking, mechanical support, and a broom wagon
BikeRadar has listed it among the UK's toughest long-distance sportives — independent, if not exhaustive, corroboration
A generous 19-hour window (05:00 to midnight) makes the distance achievable for strong but not elite riders
weaknesses: Small full-distance field relative to mainstream UK sportives — 2025 recorded only 28 full-route ultra finishers
Rocky history: the advertised 2021 inaugural edition was cancelled; the event's actual first completed edition was 2022, not the earlier founding year sometimes cited

The organizer's own web material is inconsistent — conflicting elevation figures (18,000 ft alongside both 5,500 m and an apparently miscalculated 6,000 m) and conflicting 2026 event dates across different pages
'UK's toughest sportive' is the organizer's own claim, not an independently established fact
No same-day entry — advance registration only
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A real, demanding one-day ultra with solid support infrastructure, let down by a small track record and untidy official information. Verify the current date and course details directly with the organizer before committing — this profile's own data conflicts are a symptom of the same problem.

final_verdict.one_liner: UK's definitive ultra sportive - 200 miles of Dartmoor savagery.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you want a genuine 200-mile UK ultra-distance test with real support and can tolerate a small field and some organizational untidiness. Skip if you want a large-field event or need rock-solid, consistent official information before you commit.
final_verdict.alternatives: If you want a similarly long UK ultra with a larger, more established field: LoToJa Classic-style format isn't available in the UK, but check London-Edinburgh-London for extreme distance. If you want Dartmoor terrain in a shorter package: Dartmoor Classic (same region, shorter distance).

eligibility.notes: RESOLVED (was 'unknown'): the dartmoorlegend.co.uk domain remains unreliable, but organizer Sportiva Events maintains a live event page advertising the next edition (May 8, 2027, entries open) as of 2026-07-23. Some stale 2026 copy remains on the page and the organizer's results index/date fields are internally inconsistent (July 5 vs July 11 for 2026), but the active 2027 listing supports current operating status.
